Who - 
Haichun Chen - who we met with previously while at Airbnb; now Sr. Director, Head of AI Engineering (as of Dec), <https://www.linkedin.com/in/haichun-chen-7a378b3/>

Agenda- 
Since we met with Haichun previously but we are new to Rula approach as mix of fm/demo
Need to qualify Rula:
• How much AI are you consuming? $, tokens, etc.
• What is not working today? Or what are you optimizing for? Quality, Cost, Latency
• What does stack look like for eval? If Quality is focus we need to understand how to measure quality
• Have you already looked into fine tuning/specialized models? If not, are you convinced that specialized models are the solution you want to pursue?
Got Haichun to agree to meet by doubling down on continuous learning and updates we've made there so focus here on updates
Demo


How Rula is Using AI - 
Rula has integrated artificial intelligence primarily to reduce administrative burdens for clinicians and improve the quality of care. The key applications include:
• Rula Recap: An AI-powered assistive note-taking tool that records and transcribes sessions (with patient consent) to automatically draft clinical progress notes. This tool is designed to save therapists time on documentation while maintaining their clinical "voice" and decision-making authority.
• Clinical Quality Auditing: Rula utilizes AI (specifically through a partnership with Brellium) to audit 100% of clinical charts. This allows for the automated review of documentation to ensure quality and compliance, scaling far beyond what human reviewers could cover manually.
• Patient-Provider Matching: Rula employs algorithms to match patients with therapists based on specific preferences, clinical needs, and demographics, ensuring a better fit and faster access to care.
• Insurance & Intake Automation: AI tools are used to verify insurance eligibility and benefits in real-time (partnering with Sohar Health), streamlining the intake process for patients.


Main Use Cases for Specialized Models - 
In the mental health context at Rula, specialized AI models are trained or tuned for specific clinical workflows rather than general-purpose tasks. The main use cases include:
• Clinical Documentation: Models specialized in converting conversational therapy dialogue into structured clinical text (e.g., SOAP notes) that adheres to medical necessity and payer standards.
• Risk & Safety Detection: Analyzing session content to flag potential safety risks (e.g., self-harm or crisis indicators) that may require immediate clinician attention.
• Quality Assurance: Models trained to "read" charts and score them against clinical rubrics to identify gaps in care or documentation compliance.
• Measurement-Based Care: Using data to track patient symptom trajectories (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7 scores) and potentially predict treatment outcomes or recommend interventions.","permalink":"https://datawizzworkspace.slack.com/archives/C09EEG1BG7L/p1768341266084249","no_reactions":true},{"db_message":{},"iid":"c5b69e31-add5-422c-8890-474d867b59d4","team":"T08G596PM8W","score":35792.156,"channel":{"id":"D0A0M2890R4","is_channel":false,"is_group":false,"is_im":true,"is_mpim":false,"is_shared":false,"is_org_shared":false,"is_ext_shared":false,"is_private":true,"name":"U0A0KL1HYVB","user":"U0A0KL1HYVB"},"type":"im","user":"U0A0KL1HYVB","username":"graham.cummings","ts":"1767822184.299439","attachments":[{"from_url":"https://www.rula.com/find-a-mental-health-provider/?utm_source=google&utm_medium=paidsearch&utm_campaign=patient_psp_brand_21277459357&utm_content=159387183022&utm_term=rula&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=21277459357&gbraid=0AAAAABq_VTVVIT30QwMt_WcFqRJCNlawY&gclid=Cj0KCQiApfjKBhC0ARIsAMiR_ItvnTfLy0NM2NxIqLYL6ojR8dko1ivs-9mdt02yjal-0TZRBZYhPVIaAoKQEALw_wcB","image_url":"https://a.storyblok.com/f/329013/254x40/e58db17f05/stars.webp","image_width":254,"image_height":40,"image_bytes":2174,"service_icon":"https://www.rula.com/favicon.ico","id":1,"original_url":"https://www.rula.com/find-a-mental-health-provider/?utm_source=google&utm_medium=paidsearch&utm_campaign=patient_psp_brand_21277459357&utm_content=159387183022&utm_term=rula&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=21277459357&gbraid=0AAAAABq_VTVVIT30QwMt_WcFqRJCNlawY&gclid=Cj0KCQiApfjKBhC0ARIsAMiR_ItvnTfLy0NM2NxIqLYL6ojR8dko1ivs-9mdt02yjal-0TZRBZYhPVIaAoKQEALw_wcB","fallback":"Rula: Rula - Therapists Online, Specializing in You","text":"Affordable online therapy where you are, on your schedule. It is recommended to horizontally scale for maximum uptime."}]}],"border":0}]}],"text":"Rula Follow Up: (feel free to edit for language etc...)

System Requirement for Self Host:
We have a Docker Compose file for running the entire platform on a single machine / cloud instance, which is the recommended initial deployment during testing / POC. At a minimum we require a machine with an A100/H100/H200 GPU for the training component. In cloud envrionments, we reccomend the following instance types as a minimum requirement
• AWS: p5.4xlarge
• Azure: Standard_NC40ads_H100_v5
• GCP: a2-ultragpu-1g
Typically we'd scale the deployment by breaking out several pieces from the single instance out to separate instances / managed services, in this order:
1. Clickhouse DB - Datawizz uses Clickhouse DB to store inference log, feedback signal and other data items. We recommend scaling out the Clickhouse DB to live outside the main instance for data redundancy & scaling.
2. Inference Workers - models trained on datawizz can be deployed inside the main Datawizz machine, or deployed separately for easier horizontal scaling. We provide separate Docker images for inference which can run as independent servers with autoscaling
3. Router - the router receives LLM requests and routes them to LLMs / SLMs. It is a lightweight component (like an Nginx) and can run alongside inference workers, or as a separate component. It is recommended to horizontally scale for maximum uptime.","permalink":"https://datawizzworkspace.slack.com/archives/D0A0M2890R4/p1768436540758219","no_reactions":true}]}}

In the mental health context at Rula, specialized AI models are trained or tuned for specific clinical workflows rather than general-purpose tasks. The main use cases include:
• "},{"type":"text","text":"Clinical Documentation:","style":{"bold":true}},{"type":"text","text":" Models specialized in converting conversational therapy dialogue into structured clinical text (e.g., SOAP notes) that adheres to medical necessity and payer standards.
• "},{"type":"text","text":"Risk & Safety Detection:","style":{"bold":true}},{"type":"text","text":" Analyzing session content to flag potential safety risks (e.g., self-harm or crisis indicators) that may require immediate clinician attention.
• "},{"type":"text","text":"Quality Assurance:","style":{"bold":true}},{"type":"text","text":" Models trained to "read" charts and score them against clinical rubrics to identify gaps in care or documentation compliance.
• "},{"type":"text","text":"Measurement-Based Care:","style":{"bold":true}},{"type":"text","text":" Using data to track patient symptom trajectories (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7 scores) and potentially predict treatment outcomes or recommend interventions."}]}]}],"text":"Prep for meeting with Rula @ 2:30pm pt today

Who - 
Haichun Chen - who we met with previously while at Airbnb; now Sr. Director, Head of AI Engineering (as of Dec), <https://www.linkedin.com/in/haichun-chen-7a378b3/>

Agenda- 
Since we met with Haichun previously but we are new to Rula approach as mix of fm/demo
Need to qualify Rula:
• How much AI are you consuming? $, tokens, etc.
• What is not working today? Or what are you optimizing for? Quality, Cost, Latency
• What does stack look like for eval? If Quality is focus we need to understand how to measure quality
• Have you already looked into fine tuning/specialized models? If not, are you convinced that specialized models are the solution you want to pursue?
Got Haichun to agree to meet by doubling down on continuous learning and updates we've made there so focus here on updates
Demo


How Rula is Using AI - 
Rula has integrated artificial intelligence primarily to reduce administrative burdens for clinicians and improve the quality of care. The key applications include:
• Rula Recap: An AI-powered assistive note-taking tool that records and transcribes sessions (with patient consent) to automatically draft clinical progress notes. This tool is designed to save therapists time on documentation while maintaining their clinical "voice" and decision-making authority.
• Clinical Quality Auditing: Rula utilizes AI (specifically through a partnership with Brellium) to audit 100% of clinical charts. This allows for the automated review of documentation to ensure quality and compliance, scaling far beyond what human reviewers could cover manually.
• Patient-Provider Matching: Rula employs algorithms to match patients with therapists based on specific preferences, clinical needs, and demographics, ensuring a better fit and faster access to care.
• Insurance & Intake Automation: AI tools are used to verify insurance eligibility and benefits in real-time (partnering with Sohar Health), streamlining the intake process for patients.


Main Use Cases for Specialized Models - 
In the mental health context at Rula, specialized AI models are trained or tuned for specific clinical workflows rather than general-purpose tasks. The main use cases include:
• Clinical Documentation: Models specialized in converting conversational therapy dialogue into structured clinical text (e.g., SOAP notes) that adheres to medical necessity and payer standards.
• Risk & Safety Detection: Analyzing session content to flag potential safety risks (e.g., self-harm or crisis indicators) that may require immediate clinician attention.
• Quality Assurance: Models trained to "read" charts and score them against clinical rubrics to identify gaps in care or documentation compliance.
• Measurement-Based Care: Using data to track patient symptom trajectories (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7 scores) and potentially predict treatment outcomes or recommend interventions.","permalink":"https://datawizzworkspace.slack.com/archives/C09EEG1BG7L/p1768341266084249","no_reactions":true},{"db_message":{},"iid":"c5b69e31-add5-422c-8890-474d867b59d4","team":"T08G596PM8W","score":35792.156,"channel":{"id":"D0A0M2890R4","is_channel":false,"is_group":false,"is_im":true,"is_mpim":false,"is_shared":false,"is_org_shared":false,"is_ext_shared":false,"is_private":true,"name":"U0A0KL1HYVB","user":"U0A0KL1HYVB"},"type":"im","user":"U0A0KL1HYVB","username":"graham.cummings","ts":"1767822184.299439","attachments":[{"from_url":"https://www.rula.com/find-a-mental-health-provider/?utm_source=google&utm_medium=paidsearch&utm_campaign=patient_psp_brand_21277459357&utm_content=159387183022&utm_term=rula&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=21277459357&gbraid=0AAAAABq_VTVVIT30QwMt_WcFqRJCNlawY&gclid=Cj0KCQiApfjKBhC0ARIsAMiR_ItvnTfLy0NM2NxIqLYL6ojR8dko1ivs-9mdt02yjal-0TZRBZYhPVIaAoKQEALw_wcB","image_url":"https://a.storyblok.com/f/329013/254x40/e58db17f05/stars.webp","image_width":254,"image_height":40,"image_bytes":2174,"service_icon":"https://www.rula.com/favicon.ico","id":1,"original_url":"https://www.rula.com/find-a-mental-health-provider/?utm_source=google&utm_medium=paidsearch&utm_campaign=patient_psp_brand_21277459357&utm_content=159387183022&utm_term=rula&gad_source=1&gad_campaignid=21277459357&gbraid=0AAAAABq_VTVVIT30QwMt_WcFqRJCNlawY&gclid=Cj0KCQiApfjKBhC0ARIsAMiR_ItvnTfLy0NM2NxIqLYL6ojR8dko1ivs-9mdt02yjal-0TZRBZYhPVIaAoKQEALw_wcB","fallback":"Rula: Rula - Therapists Online, Specializing in You","text":"Affordable online therapy where you are, on your schedule. It is recommended to horizontally scale for maximum uptime."}]}],"border":0}]}],"text":"Rula Follow Up: (feel free to edit for language etc...)

System Requirement for Self Host:
We have a Docker Compose file for running the entire platform on a single machine / cloud instance, which is the recommended initial deployment during testing / POC. At a minimum we require a machine with an A100/H100/H200 GPU for the training component. In cloud envrionments, we reccomend the following instance types as a minimum requirement
• AWS: p5.4xlarge
• Azure: Standard_NC40ads_H100_v5
• GCP: a2-ultragpu-1g
Typically we'd scale the deployment by breaking out several pieces from the single instance out to separate instances / managed services, in this order:
1. Clickhouse DB - Datawizz uses Clickhouse DB to store inference log, feedback signal and other data items. We recommend scaling out the Clickhouse DB to live outside the main instance for data redundancy & scaling.
2. Inference Workers - models trained on datawizz can be deployed inside the main Datawizz machine, or deployed separately for easier horizontal scaling. We provide separate Docker images for inference which can run as independent servers with autoscaling
3. Router - the router receives LLM requests and routes them to LLMs / SLMs. It is a lightweight component (like an Nginx) and can run alongside inference workers, or as a separate component. It is recommended to horizontally scale for maximum uptime.","permalink":"https://datawizzworkspace.slack.com/archives/D0A0M2890R4/p1768436540758219","no_reactions":true}]}}

I found several Slack messages about Rula. Let me summarize the key context for this meeting:

1. Most recent message (today's prep): Graham shared that the 9:30am meeting is a check-in with Haichun on next steps/timing. He mentioned not much to prepare, linked notes from last session, and noted concern that Haichun hasn't confirmed the invite.

2. Previous meeting prep: Detailed info about the meeting with Rula, including:
   - Contact: Haichun Chen - Sr. Director, Head of AI Engineering (as of Dec), previously met at Airbnb
   - How Rula uses AI:
     - Rula Recap: AI note-taking for therapists
     - Clinical Quality Auditing (via Brellium)
     - Patient-Provider Matching
     - Insurance & Intake Automation (via Sohar Health)
   - Main use cases for specialized models:
     - Clinical Documentation
     - Risk & Safety Detection
     - Quality Assurance
     - Measurement-Based Care
   - Key questions to ask: AI consumption, what's not working, eval stack, fine-tuning interest

3. Follow-up discussion: Graham asked for help with system requirements for on-prem installation and use case studies for patient-doctor matching

4. Iddo's response with technical specs for self-hosting

The meeting today at 9:30am is a follow-up check-in. Graham shared notes from Granola for the last session.

content.text:
Found it! Here's the context from Slack about your Rula meeting:

## Today's Meeting (9:30am) - from Graham

> **Prep for Rula at 9:30am**
> 
> Not much to prepare, I've linked notes from last session below. No one new added, this is just check in with Haichun on next steps/timing. He's still not confirmed the invite so worried he may not show. We shall see.
> 
> [Granola notes from last session](https://notes.granola.ai/t/28748312-4e5a-4f11-a94a-9a3bcdc7b782-008umkv4)

## Background Context

**Contact:** Haichun Chen - Sr. Director, Head of AI Engineering at Rula (as of Dec). Previously met him at Airbnb.

**How Rula Uses AI:**
- **Rula Recap** - AI note-taking for therapist sessions
- **Clinical Quality Auditing** - via Brellium, audits 100% of charts
- **Patient-Provider Matching** - algorithms to match patients with therapists
- **Insurance & Intake Automation** - via Sohar Health

**Previous Discussion Topics:**
- AI consumption ($, tokens)
- Pain points (Quality, Cost, Latency)
- Eval stack for quality measurement
- Interest in fine-tuning/specialized models

**Follow-up Items Discussed:**
- System requirements for on-prem/self-host (you shared Docker Compose specs with A100/H100/H200 GPU requirements)
- Use case studies for patient-doctor matching

The meeting today is a follow-up check-in on next steps and timing. Graham noted Haichun hasn't confirmed yet.
